zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hollis於1540年出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Robert Hollis20歲，母親Ursala Cooper15歲。他於1564年與Sarah Lees結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他於1544年住在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩且於1600年住在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩聖 安德魯。他去世於1600年3月13日在英國英格蘭倫敦霍爾波恩聖 安德魯 霍爾波恩 阿波維 馮 巴爾斯 煙雨 聖 喬治 馮 馬迪爾享年60歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可德雷哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Hollis于1540年出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲Robert Hollis20岁，母亲Ursala Cooper15岁。他于1564年与Sarah Lees结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他于1544年住在英国米德萊可霍爾波恩且于1600年住在英国米德萊可霍爾波恩聖 安德魯。他去世于1600年3月13日在英国英格兰倫敦霍爾波恩聖 安德魯 霍爾波恩 阿波維 馮 巴爾斯 煙雨 聖 喬治 馮 馬迪爾享年60岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可德雷哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Hollis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) holly or holly trees’, from Middle English holi(e), holin (Old English holegn, holen). Compare Hollen and Holley 3.

variants.name: Holley
meanings: nickname from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + eie, egh(e) ‘eye’ (Old English hol + ēage), perhaps a nickname for someone with a missing eye.
topographic name for someone who lived by a ‘clearing in a hollow’, from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + lei(e) ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Hollins
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hollins (Staffordshire, Lancashire, Yorkshire), or else a topographic name from the plural of Middle English holin ‘holly’ (see Hollis ).

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Hollen
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a holly tree, from Middle English holin ‘holly’ (Old English holegn). Compare Hollis .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Hollis was born in 1540, in Essex, England, his father, Robert Hollis, was 20 and his mother, Ursala Cooper, was 15. He married Sarah Lees in 1564. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He lived in Holborn, Middlesex, England in 1544 and St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, England in 1600. He died on 13 March 1600, in St Andrew Holborn Above the Bars with St George the Martyr, Holborn, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Dereham, Norfolk, England.

Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il a vécu à Holborn, Middlesex, Angleterre en 1544 et à St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, Angleterre en 1600. Il est décédé le 13 mars 1600, à St Andrew Holborn Above the Bars with St George the Martyr, Holborn, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Dereham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

ko.St. Andrew, Holburn, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 홀본 스트 앤드루 홀본 압브 테 바스 위스 스트 조지 테 마티르
ko.lifeSummary: William Hollis(이)가 1540년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Hollis(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Ursala Cooper(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1564년 Sarah Lees(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1544년도에 영국 미들레세스 홀본에서, 1600년도에 영국 미들레세스 홀본 스트 앤드루에서 살았다. 그는 1600년 3월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 홀본 스트 앤드루 홀본 압브 테 바스 위스 스트 조지 테 마티르에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 더함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Hollis nasceu em 1540, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Hollis, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Ursala Cooper, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Lees em 1564. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele viveu em Holborn, Middlesex, Inglaterra em 1544 e em St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, Inglaterra em 1600. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Hollis родился в 1540 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Hollis было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Ursala Cooper, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Lees в 1564 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он жил в местности холборн, миддлесекс, Англия в 1544 году и в местности ст андрю, холборн, миддлесекс, Англия в 1600 году. Он умер 13 марта 1600 г. в местности ст андрю холборн абов т барс вит ст джеордж т мартир, холборн, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности дирэам, норфолк, Англия.
